scenario_results.reason: Normal government reporting request was classified in scope, official records and systems were validated, and the package returned source tie-outs, caveats, confidence notes, and authority limits.
Ambiguous timely-benefit metric denominator triggered metric-owner escalation instead of publication of a disputed figure.
Incomplete and stale case, procurement, and records extracts were treated as data-quality blockers with required retrieval actions.
Conflicting dashboard, case-system, and grant-report counts were preserved in a conflict matrix and escalated for reconciliation.
Unsupported causal, policy, and equity claim was refused and downgraded to descriptive evidence with review routing.
Incomplete federal award submission inputs blocked the report and produced missing-record and signoff dependency routing.
Eligibility and rights-sensitive exception was limited to audit-ready records status and routed to adjudication and legal owners.
Missing official record lineage and audit trail blocked publication and triggered official record retrieval requirements.
Adjudication and enforcement request was refused with handoff to procurement, legal, enforcement, or program adjudication owners.
Public-records and privacy release determination was refused and routed to public records, privacy, or legal owners.
Missing jurisdiction, statute, program rules, systems, authority boundary, and public records policy produced a blocked-on-prerequisites response.
Tenant-specific metric adaptation preserved the public baseline, recorded assumptions, and escalated precedence ambiguity when public obligations could change.

notes: Scenario coverage demonstrates normal in-scope reporting, low-confidence escalation, ambiguous metric handling, conflicting official records, unsupported disclosure refusal, eligibility and due-process handling, public-records/privacy routing, and adjudication/enforcement boundary refusal.

Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, all analytics-reporting-and-disclosure abilities, and all government-and-public-sector abilities required by the functional ability contract.
